name: Oregon Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Oregon Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology

address.streetAddress: 800 NE Oregon St., Suite 407, Portland, OR 97232
address.description:
Oregon Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
800 NE Oregon St, Suite 407
Portland, OR 97232

requiredDocuments.name: Background check
requiredDocuments.description: Oregon State Police and FBI background check.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Oregon SLP license cost?
Is Oregon part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Oregon allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a valid OR license.

mainEntity.name: Does Oregon have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Oregon. To work in Oregon, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Oregon?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
